user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Daily automation: Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected. Account: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-17 13:44 EST
Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with escalation triage. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
Date: 2024-02-13 12:26 EST
Subject: escalation triage - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with escalation triage. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-23 15:29 EST
Subject: escalation triage - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about escalation triage. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery
Date: 2024-02-04
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), Priya (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery)

[00:02:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on escalation triage from our last session.

[00:08:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:12:00] Priya (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:14:00] Priya (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:18:00] Priya (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:21:00] Priya (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:25:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on performance requirements and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:28:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on budget approval and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:32:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:34:00] Priya (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:36:00] Priya (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.
